data.body: PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness) review: clean. The recorder-step-timing approach is grounded and sound: the interpreter already records per-step duration_ms (interpreter.py:388/489), the batch finder's criteria_count is carried in its recorded outputs (production_batch_runner.py:134), and gate_dispatch reconstructs the tier split tolerantly (gate_dispatch.py:141-171). Mechanism achieves the named goal (reinstate coverage['metrics']), not a proxy; partial/untimed records and the coach-failure recovery path are both handled. Considered an alternative (per-call timing wrappers) — rejected as more invasive vs reusing the existing recorder timings, so the chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on blast-radius/codebase-alignment. Positive rationale is stated in Approach. No findings.
